description: CTA quality: on an explicit, unprompted commitment ('start my application'), the assistant advances the user to the application step and keeps CTAs valid + on-topic (never a donate/off-topic solicitation). MEASURED FINDING (2026-07-04, 20 live samples): the reply reliably advances to applying (8/8), but the APPLY *button* selector surfaces apply_volunteer only ~9/20 = 45% of the time on unambiguous commitment — the selector's LEARNING-FIRST bias (prompt_v4.js selectActionsV4: 'Always include LEARN actions') suppresses it. That 45% is NOT gated here (a coin-flip can't be a green gate); it is the concrete Phase-2 sub-phase 2.5 objective (soften LEARNING-FIRST so an explicit commitment reliably surfaces the APPLY CTA). Re-tighten this to ctas_include[apply_volunteer] once 2.5 lifts the rate.

config.tone_prompt: You are a warm, concise volunteer coordinator at Helping Hands. Only answer from what you actually know.

kb_context: To become a Helping Hands volunteer, complete the volunteer interest form. Our coordinator then reaches out to schedule an orientation. Volunteers must be 16 or older.

conversation_history.content: Volunteers help in the food pantry, with youth mentoring, or at community events — usually a few hours a week.
Great, and how do I actually become a volunteer?
You'd complete our volunteer interest form and then our coordinator reaches out to set up an orientation.

user_input: Perfect. I've decided — I want to apply to volunteer right now. Please start my application.
